task_name: Add Local MVP Chain Checkpoint Through Auth Persistence Readiness
goal: Document the current local MVP chain through Auth/Persistence Readiness Candidate and define the next safe boundary without enabling auth, secrets, persistence, approval recording, task creation, execution, or live Builder access.
claims: BP-066 documents the local MVP chain through Auth/Persistence Readiness Candidate.
BP-066 identifies which chain stages are locally reviewable and which actions remain blocked.
BP-066 does not implement approval, auth, secrets, persistence, UI, Builder task creation, execution, DB, deploy, or live Builder access.
assumptions: BP-065 completed fixture coverage visibility for the Auth/Persistence Readiness Candidate stage.
A checkpoint is needed before expanding toward Builder Task Create readiness or any real auth/persistence work.
The next safe step should remain a contract-only boundary.
dod: The document states the local chain stages, current review suite entry point, blocked actions, open gaps, and next safe step.
The document explicitly blocks approval effect, approval recording, auth implementation, secrets, persistence, Task Create, Execute, Approve, Push, Deploy, DB, and live Builder calls.
stop_conditions: Runtime, fixture, package, or tool changes become necessary
Auth implementation, secrets, persistence, approval recording, live Builder, DB, deploy, execution, task creation, approve, or push becomes necessary
prior_task_findings: BP-065 made Auth/Persistence Readiness Candidate fixture coverage visible and cleared the way for a local MVP chain checkpoint.
